text.title: AI Agent Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every pattern+framework selection enforces.

rule.statement: The task is classified in writing on three axes — needs_tools (bool), step_count (int), branching (bool) — before any pattern is named.

rationale.source: LangChain agent design guide; production cookbooks 2026

rule.rationale: Implicit classification produces pattern-by-vibes. Written classification gives a defensible decision the next maintainer can audit.

rule.statement: The chosen pattern is written as a comment at the top of the agent implementation file. Reviewers see the intent before any code.
rule.rationale: Buried pattern choice = invisible architecture decision; changes drift away from the stated intent within weeks.

rule.statement: The framework is named AND pinned to an exact version in requirements (e.g. langgraph==0.2.34). Floating dependencies of agent frameworks break production weekly.
rule.rationale: Agent frameworks ship breaking changes in minor versions; pinning is the only stable contract.

rule.statement: Every tool used by the agent has name + one-sentence description + JSON schema with required fields + deterministic JSON output (not prose).

rationale.source: OpenAI function-calling guide; Anthropic tool-use docs

rule.rationale: Vague tool descriptions are the #1 cause of wrong-tool-selected failures. Schemas are the contract.

rule.statement: The loop has max_steps (default 15) AND a loop-detection guard (break when last 3 (action, args_hash) tuples are identical). Unbounded loops are not shipped.

rationale.source: LangGraph state-machine docs; production incident postmortems

rule.rationale: Without these guards, ambiguous tool returns produce infinite loops that drain quota within hours.

example.title: Top-of-file pattern comment
example.code: # Pattern: ReAct (Reason + Act loop) # Framework: raw anthropic SDK with tool_use # Decision record: docs/agent-decisions/research-agent.json # Max steps: 15, loop-detection: on from anthropic import Anthropic ...

example.title: No pattern stated
example.code: from langgraph import StateGraph graph = StateGraph() # what pattern? what framework version? ...
example.note: Reviewer cannot verify intent matches code; framework version floats.
